title: Aspidium auriculatum
intro: Aspidium auriculatum, also known as the ear-leaved spleenwort, is a species of fern commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Aspidium auriculatum include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aspidium auriculatum is often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Aspidium auriculatum is classified within the family Polypodiaceae. However, the classification of Aspidium auriculatum may have undergone changes as the classification system has evolved over time. Aspidium auriculatum is often included in the genus Phlebodium as Phlebodium auriculatum, but it is also sometimes placed in the genus Drymoglossum.
